description: Scoring rubric for the investor news digest produced by the News Aggregator Agent.
quality_bar: Signal-to-noise ratio. Every signal in the digest must be there for a reason.

structure.tier_A: Individual signal quality (pipeline accuracy) — S1 through S8
structure.tier_B: Digest assembly quality (output quality) — D1 through D4
structure.separation_of_concerns: A failure should never be penalized on more than one axis.

context_tags.note: Alert mode evaluates only FUNDING, M&A, team_change. Digest mode evaluates the full pipeline. Signals mode has no minimum score filter.
Shorter windows reduce available signals and affect recall expectations.
Full = all APIs returned data. Partial = 1+ APIs timed out or returned empty. Degraded = majority of calls failed. Record which APIs were affected.
Larger watchlists increase curation difficulty and risk of misattribution.
When LLM is disabled, all synopses are template-based. Synopsis hallucination checks are N/A.
Lower thresholds let in more noise; higher thresholds can risk missing important signals.
When embeddings are unavailable, classification falls back to hash-based pseudo-embeddings. Score classification dimension with more leniency.

global_scale.label: Excellent
global_scale.definition: Every featured signal is relevant, correctly classified, properly sourced. No false positives in the digest view. Major events for all portfolio companies are captured. Synopsis quality is good and investment-relevant.

global_scale.label: Good
global_scale.definition: Usable with minor issues. 1-2 borderline signals included or one notable signal buried below the fold. All critical events captured. Minor classification or sentiment error on a non-featured signal.

global_scale.label: Acceptable
global_scale.definition: Covers the important events but has noticeable quality gaps: a duplicate not merged, a competitor signal outranking a similar portfolio signal, or a synopsis that's too vague. Investor would need to cross-reference.

global_scale.label: Below Standard
global_scale.definition: Significant problems: multiple misattributed signals, a major event missing from featured stories, homepage URL in the digest, or widespread classification errors. Not trustworthy without manual review.

global_scale.label: Poor
global_scale.definition: Fundamental failure: wrong company signals dominate the digest, portfolio company with major news has zero signals, hallucinated synopsis content, or digest is empty/structurally broken.

severity_matrix.description: Calibrates deduction severity across all dimensions when a signal is missed or mishandled.

dimensions.name: Signal Attribution Accuracy
dimensions.description: Is each signal correctly attributed to the right company? A wrong-company signal is worse than a missing signal.
dimensions.hard_failure: Any Featured Story attributed to the wrong company = automatic S1 = 1 and composite = 0 (triggers HF2).

anchors.anchor: 100% of signals in the digest are correctly attributed to their target company. No cross-industry name collisions leaked. Known ambiguous names (Sana, Merge) handled correctly.
anchors.example: Digest for 'Sana Labs' contains zero articles about Sana Biotechnology.

anchors.anchor: 99%+ attribution accuracy. One borderline signal where the article mentions the target company only tangentially (appears in a competitor list, not as the subject).
anchors.example: Article titled 'Top 10 AI startups' includes the target company in a list; signal is attributed but article isn't really 'about' the company.

anchors.anchor: 95%+ accuracy. 1-2 signals attributed to the wrong company, but errors are in non-featured positions and involve genuinely ambiguous names.
anchors.example: One signal about 'Merge Labs' (BCI company) leaks into the digest for 'Merge' (API platform) in the More Headlines section.

anchors.anchor: 90-94% accuracy. Multiple misattributed signals, or one misattributed signal in the Featured Stories section.
anchors.example: A featured story about 'Stripe' is actually about 'Stripe Climate' (a different product vertical), leading to a misleading synopsis.

anchors.anchor: Below 90% accuracy, or a Featured Story is about an entirely wrong company. Hard failure.
anchors.example: The #1 featured story for a portfolio company is actually about a competitor with a similar name.

dimensions.name: Filter Quality (Noise Rejection)
dimensions.description: Did the filter stage correctly remove homepages, press releases, Wikipedia, listicles, stale articles, and non-article URLs? Any of these appearing in the digest is a filter failure.
dimensions.hard_failure: Homepage URL in Featured Stories = automatic HF4.

anchors.anchor: Zero noise signals in the digest. All homepage URLs, press releases, Wikipedia articles, listicles, and stale articles were correctly filtered.
anchors.example: No signals with URLs matching /category/, /tag/, /press-releases/, or known non-article patterns.

anchors.anchor: Zero noise in Featured Stories or More Headlines. One borderline item in industry trends.
anchors.example: A Crunchbase profile page appears at position 11 in a long signal list but not in the digest view.

anchors.anchor: One noise signal leaked into More Headlines (not Featured). Or one stale article (>7 days) appears in the digest.
anchors.example: A 'Press Release: Company X Announces...' article appears at position 6 in More Headlines.

anchors.anchor: Multiple noise signals in the digest, or one noise signal in Featured Stories.
anchors.example: A Wikipedia article about the company appears as Featured Story #3.

anchors.anchor: 3+ non-article URLs, press releases, or stale signals in the digest. Hard failure if homepage URL appears in Featured.
anchors.example: Homepage URL (stripe.com with no path) appears as a featured story.

dimensions.name: Classification Accuracy
dimensions.description: Is each signal assigned the correct signal type? A FUNDING article classified as PRODUCT distorts priority ordering.
dimensions.hard_failure: A FUNDING/M&A signal classified as GENERAL and consequently excluded from the digest entirely = hard failure.

anchors.anchor: All signals in the digest have the correct signal type. Rule-based classifications match the article content. Embedding-based classifications are appropriate.
anchors.example: 'Company raises $50M Series B' correctly classified as FUNDING with confidence 0.95.

anchors.anchor: One minor misclassification on a non-featured signal where the distinction is ambiguous (e.g., PARTNERSHIP vs. CUSTOMER for a joint venture announcement).
anchors.example: A 'strategic partnership' that includes a customer deal component is classified as PARTNERSHIP instead of CUSTOMER. Reasonable ambiguity.

anchors.anchor: One misclassification on a featured signal (e.g., a CEO hire classified as GENERAL instead of HIRING), or 2-3 misclassifications on non-featured signals.
anchors.example: CEO appointment article classified as GENERAL because rule patterns didn't match the phrasing 'elevated to CEO.'

anchors.anchor: Multiple featured signals misclassified, or a CRITICAL-severity signal type is wrong (FUNDING classified as PRODUCT).
anchors.example: 'Company acquired by BigCorp for $2B' classified as PARTNERSHIP instead of M&A.

anchors.anchor: Widespread misclassification (>20% of digest signals wrong). Or a FUNDING/M&A signal classified as GENERAL and consequently scored below the digest threshold, making it invisible. Hard failure.
anchors.example: A $100M Series C for a portfolio company is classified GENERAL, scored at 35, and excluded from the digest entirely.

dimensions.name: Deduplication Quality
dimensions.description: Are same-event articles correctly merged? Are distinct events incorrectly merged? Source selection for primary URL matters.

anchors.anchor: All same-event articles merged. No false merges. Primary URL selected from highest-quality source.
anchors.example: TechCrunch, Bloomberg, and Reuters articles about the same $50M raise are merged. TechCrunch selected as primary (highest priority score).

anchors.anchor: All merges correct. One primary URL selection is not optimal but the correct article is still accessible.
anchors.example: Primary URL is from a lesser-known outlet when TechCrunch also covered the story, due to URL normalization selecting the blog's cleaner URL.

anchors.anchor: One duplicate not merged (same event appears twice in the digest), or one false merge (two distinct events combined into one signal).
anchors.example: Reuters and Bloomberg articles about the same funding round appear as separate signals in More Headlines.

anchors.anchor: Multiple duplicates visible in the digest. Or a false merge combined a funding announcement with an unrelated product launch because title similarity exceeded 60%.
anchors.example: Two featured stories are actually the same M&A announcement from different outlets.

anchors.anchor: Deduplication essentially non-functional: 3+ duplicate events in Featured Stories, or critical false merges that erase distinct signals.
anchors.example: Featured Stories #1 and #2 are the same event. A funding announcement was merged with a layoff article because both headlines mentioned the company name.

dimensions.name: Scoring & Ranking Accuracy
dimensions.description: Are signals ranked in the correct order? Incorrect ranking buries important signals below less important ones.

anchors.anchor: Featured stories are the 3 most important signals. Portfolio companies correctly prioritized. Recency bonus properly applied. Score breakdowns are consistent with the documented formula.
anchors.example: Portfolio company's $50M funding (rank=95) correctly featured above competitor's product launch (rank=65).

anchors.anchor: Ranking is correct for Featured Stories. One minor ordering issue in More Headlines.
anchors.example: More Headlines has a minor recency inversion between two MEDIUM-severity signals.

anchors.anchor: One Featured Story is arguably not top-3. Or a competitor signal of medium importance outranks a portfolio signal of equal importance.
anchors.example: A competitor's product launch is Featured Story #3 when a portfolio company's partnership announcement (same day, similar importance) is in More Headlines.

anchors.anchor: A clearly low-importance signal (MARKET or GENERAL) appears in Featured Stories while a CRITICAL-severity signal is buried in More Headlines.
anchors.example: GENERAL industry overview article featured while a portfolio company's Series C is at position 7.

anchors.anchor: Ranking is random. Critical portfolio signals missing from top positions. Score inflation.
anchors.example: A competitor's listicle mention (rank=85 due to scoring bug) outranks a portfolio company's acquisition announcement.

dimensions.name: Sentiment Accuracy
dimensions.description: Is the sentiment label (positive/negative/neutral) correct for each signal? Lower weight because sentiment is supplementary information and not a primary decision driver.

anchors.anchor: All signals have correct sentiment. Funding correctly marked positive. Layoffs correctly marked negative. Ambiguous events correctly marked neutral.
One minor sentiment error on a genuinely ambiguous signal (e.g., 'acquisition' marked neutral when it could be positive or negative depending on context).
1-2 sentiment errors on non-featured signals. Or a featured signal's sentiment is correct but the wrong intensity (a massive funding round marked neutral instead of positive).
A featured signal has inverted sentiment (layoff article marked positive, or funding article marked negative).
Widespread sentiment errors (>30% wrong). Or a portfolio company's critical negative event (data breach) is marked positive.

dimensions.name: Synopsis Quality (LLM-Enhanced)
dimensions.description: Applies only when LLM mode is enabled and synopsis was LLM-generated (featured stories with confidence ≥ 0.7). If LLM is disabled, score N/A and redistribute weight equally to S8 and D1.
dimensions.hard_failure: Any hallucinated fact in an LLM synopsis = S7 = 1 and automatic flag. If the hallucinated synopsis appears in a Featured Story, composite = 0 (triggers HF1).

anchors.anchor: Synopsis is 1-2 sentences. States what happened, why it matters, and includes key numbers. Every claim is traceable to the headline/excerpt. Investment-relevant framing and concise.
anchors.example: 'Stripe raised a $50M Series B led by Sequoia, bringing total funding to $75M. The round suggests strong enterprise traction following a 3x revenue year.' (Numbers match excerpt.)

anchors.anchor: Synopsis is accurate and concise. Slightly generic framing but no hallucination. All facts traceable.
anchors.example: Synopsis accurately describes the event but says 'this could be meaningful for the space' instead of citing a specific metric from the excerpt.

anchors.anchor: Synopsis is accurate but reads as a paraphrase of the headline (low synthesis effort). Or is 3-4 sentences (slightly too long).
anchors.example: Synopsis essentially restates the headline with minor wording changes and no investment framing.

anchors.anchor: Synopsis contains one claim not clearly supported by the headline/excerpt (borderline hallucination). Or is an echo of the input.
anchors.example: Synopsis mentions 'a $2B valuation' when the excerpt only says 'raised $50M' with no valuation mentioned.

anchors.anchor: Synopsis contains a fabricated fact (clear hallucination). Automatic hard failure.
anchors.example: Synopsis claims 'the acquisition was completed for $500M' when the article only mentions 'acquisition talks.'

dimensions.name: Synopsis Quality (Template-Based)
dimensions.description: Template synopses have near-zero hallucination risk but can be too vague or fail to extract key details.

anchors.anchor: Template correctly populated all slots: company name, amount, series, context. Synopsis is specific and informative despite being formulaic.
anchors.example: 'Stripe raised $50M in Series B funding. The round was led by Sequoia Capital.' (All slots filled from article data.)

anchors.anchor: Template mostly populated. One optional slot missing but core information present.
anchors.example: 'Stripe raised $50M in Series B funding.' (Lead investor not extracted but available in excerpt.)

anchors.anchor: Template produced a vague result because extraction failed on key details. Synopsis is technically correct but uninformative.
anchors.example: 'Stripe announced funding details.' (Amount and series not extracted.)

anchors.anchor: Template produced a near-empty synopsis for a signal where rich data was available in the excerpt.
anchors.example: 'Acme announced details.' for a $100M Series C with extensive excerpt data.

anchors.anchor: Template synopsis is factually wrong (wrong amount extracted, wrong company name inserted) or completely empty.
anchors.example: Template says 'raised $5M' when excerpt clearly states '$50M.' (Regex extraction error.)

dimensions.name: Digest Precision
dimensions.description: What percentage of signals in the digest view are truly relevant to the target companies? Evaluated on the full digest (Featured + More Headlines + Industry Trends).
dimensions.hard_failure: Precision < 70% = digest is more noise than signal. Hard failure.

anchors.anchor: Precision ≥ 95%. Every signal in the digest is clearly about the correct company, is a genuine news article (not noise), and is within the date range.
anchors.example: 20 signals in digest; 20/20 are relevant, correctly attributed, and timely.

anchors.anchor: Precision 90-94%. 1-2 borderline signals (tangential mention, or company appears only in a list within the article).
anchors.example: 20 signals; 18-19 clearly relevant; 1-2 are 'about' the company but only tangentially.

anchors.anchor: Precision 80-89%. 3-4 irrelevant or borderline signals. Noise is visible but concentrated in More Headlines, not Featured.
anchors.example: 20 signals; 4 are press releases or tangential articles that passed the filter.

anchors.anchor: Precision 70-79%. Noise is visible in Featured Stories or comprises >20% of digest.
anchors.example: 15 signals; 4 are about wrong companies or are non-article URLs.

anchors.anchor: Precision < 70%. The digest is more noise than signal. Investor cannot trust it. Hard failure.
anchors.example: 10 signals; 4 are about wrong companies, 2 are Wikipedia articles.

dimensions.name: Digest Recall
dimensions.description: What percentage of major real-world events surfaced? Evaluated against the severity matrix. Critical events for portfolio companies must be captured. Requires external verification for D2.
dimensions.data_availability_note: If Parallel Search did not return any articles about an event (API Coverage = Partial/Degraded), the missed event is a data availability issue, not a digest quality failure. Record in context tags and do not penalize D2.
dimensions.hard_failure: Critical portfolio event was missed entirely despite being available in the API data.

anchors.anchor: Recall ≥ 90%. All critical events for portfolio companies captured. All high events for portfolio and competitors captured. At most 1 medium event missed.
anchors.example: Portfolio company announced Series C (captured), competitor had layoffs (captured), portfolio company hired new CTO (captured).

anchors.anchor: Recall 80-89%. All critical events captured. 1-2 high events missed (e.g., a competitor's VP hire not surfaced).
anchors.example: All funding and M&A events captured; one competitor's product launch missed because Parallel Search didn't return it.

anchors.anchor: Recall 70-79%. One critical event for a competitor missed, or 2-3 high events missed. All critical portfolio events captured.
anchors.example: Competitor's $200M acquisition missed entirely. But all portfolio company events captured.

anchors.anchor: Recall 60-69%. One critical event for a portfolio company missed (but the API returned the article and it was filtered incorrectly).
anchors.example: Portfolio company's Series B was in the raw fetch but filtered out because the company name appeared only in the excerpt, not the headline.

anchors.anchor: Recall < 60%, or a critical portfolio event was missed entirely despite being available in the API data. Hard failure.
anchors.example: Portfolio company announced acquisition; Parallel Search returned the TechCrunch article; filter excluded it. Investor learned about it from Twitter instead.

dimensions.name: Digest Ordering & Curation
dimensions.description: Are Featured Stories the right 3? Is portfolio prioritized over competitors of equal importance? Is recency reflected?

anchors.anchor: Featured Stories are unambiguously the 3 most important signals. Portfolio signals ranked above competitor signals of equal importance. Recency properly reflected. Industry Trends section adds useful context. Overview stats are accurate.
Featured Stories are correct. One minor curation issue: a competitor signal ranked above a portfolio signal of similar (not identical) importance, or Industry Trends section is thin.
One Featured Story is arguably not top-3 (a more important signal is in More Headlines). Or portfolio/competitor prioritization is inconsistent. Or overview stats are slightly off.
Featured Stories include a clearly low-priority signal while a CRITICAL event is buried. Or the digest is competitor-heavy when portfolio companies had significant news.
Featured Stories are essentially random. No coherent prioritization. Portfolio company with major news has zero featured signals while competitors dominate.

dimensions.name: Structural & Format Compliance
dimensions.description: Does the digest follow the documented Markdown format? Are source links present? Dates present? Section limits respected?

anchors.anchor: All sections present (Overview, Featured Stories, More Headlines, optionally Industry Trends). Source URLs present and functional for every signal. Published dates present. Section caps respected (≤3 featured, ≤8 more headlines, ≤5 industries). Generation timestamp present.
All sections present. One minor formatting issue (e.g., sentiment icon missing, date format inconsistent). All source URLs present.
One non-critical section missing (e.g., Industry Trends). Or 1-2 signals missing source URLs. Or section caps slightly exceeded.
Multiple format violations: missing source URLs for featured stories, no overview stats, or significantly exceeded section caps.
Digest is structurally broken: missing Featured Stories section, empty output, or output in wrong format (JSON instead of Markdown for --digest mode).

hard_failure_conditions.name: Synopsis Hallucination
hard_failure_conditions.definition: An LLM-generated synopsis contains a factual claim (number, date, company name, event) not present in the article headline or excerpt. A single instance in a Featured Story is sufficient.

hard_failure_conditions.name: Featured Wrong-Company
hard_failure_conditions.definition: A Featured Story (top 3) is attributed to the wrong company. The article is clearly about a different entity than the target company.

hard_failure_conditions.name: Portfolio CRITICAL Miss
hard_failure_conditions.definition: A CRITICAL-severity event (FUNDING, M&A, IPO, SECURITY, LEGAL, LAYOFFS) for a portfolio company was present in the raw API data but absent from the digest due to a pipeline failure (filter, classification, or scoring error).

hard_failure_conditions.name: Homepage in Digest
hard_failure_conditions.definition: A homepage URL (domain with no article path) appears anywhere in the digest view (Featured or More Headlines).

hard_failure_conditions.name: Empty Digest
hard_failure_conditions.definition: The digest is empty, truncated, or structurally broken (no parseable sections) when the raw data contained signals above the minimum threshold.

failure_mode_severity_mapping.impact: S1 = 1. If in Featured, HF2 → composite = 0.
S2 deduct 2. If in Featured, HF4 → composite = 0.
S2 deduct 1-2 per noise signal in digest.
S2 deduct 1 per stale signal. Featured stale = deduct 2.
S4 deduct 1 per unmerged duplicate. Featured duplicate = deduct 2.
S3 deduct 1-2 per misclassification. CRITICAL type misclassified = deduct 2.
Note in scoring. Do not penalize if rule-based classification was correct.
S6 deduct 1 per mismatch. Inverted sentiment on Featured = deduct 2.
S5 deduct 1-2 per inflation that affected Featured selection.
S5 deduct 1. D3 deduct 1 if it displaced a portfolio Featured story.
S5 deduct 0.5 per inversion in same-importance tier.
S7 = 1. If in Featured, HF1 → composite = 0.
S7 deduct 2. Verbatim echo is a synthesis failure.
S7 or S8 deduct 1. Vague but accurate is better than hallucinated.
Deduct 0.5. Minor formatting concern.
D4 deduct 1 per missing link. Featured missing link = deduct 2.
D4 deduct 0.5 per missing date.
D3 deduct 1 per misattributed industry tag.
D2 deduct 1-2 depending on competitor's significance.
If portfolio company: HF3 if events existed. D2 = 1.
S8 deduct 0.5. Expected limitation of template approach.
S2 deduct 1 if it caused a valid article to be excluded.

edge_cases.scenario: Company has no news in the time window (API returned 0 articles)
edge_cases.correct_behavior: Digest omits the company or notes 'No recent signals.' This is correct behavior when data is unavailable.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: Do not penalize D2. Record API Coverage = Partial. If ALL companies have 0 signals, investigate API failure.

edge_cases.scenario: Cross-industry name collision: filter correctly excludes wrong-company article
edge_cases.correct_behavior: Article is filtered out. No signal generated. This is correct.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: S1 = 5 and S2 = 5 for this event. Do not penalize recall — the article was correctly identified as irrelevant.

edge_cases.scenario: Cross-industry name collision: filter FAILS and includes wrong-company article
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: S1 deducted. If in Featured, HF2 triggered.

edge_cases.scenario: Featured story has confidence = 0.65 (below 0.7 LLM threshold), classified correctly by rules
edge_cases.correct_behavior: Signal gets template synopsis instead of LLM synopsis. Classification is correct. Expected behavior.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: Do not penalize S3 or S7. Score S8 based on template quality.

edge_cases.scenario: Deduplicator selects lower-quality source as primary due to URL normalization
edge_cases.correct_behavior: The correct event is represented but with a suboptimal source link.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: S4 = 4 (merge was correct, source selection was suboptimal). D4 not penalized unless source link is broken.

edge_cases.scenario: A competitor's FUNDING announcement is #1 story when a portfolio company's PRODUCT launch also occurred
edge_cases.correct_behavior: FUNDING (+40 importance) should outrank PRODUCT (+20) regardless of company category, because signal importance > company type in the scoring formula.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: This is CORRECT behavior per the scoring formula. S5 = 5. Do not penalize for competitor outranking portfolio when the signal type importance differential is large.

edge_cases.scenario: --min-priority set to 60 by investor, causing a portfolio PARTNERSHIP signal (rank=55) to be excluded
edge_cases.correct_behavior: Signal correctly excluded per investor's threshold configuration.
edge_cases.scoring_guidance: Record min-priority threshold in context tags. Do not penalize D2 for signals excluded by explicit investor configuration.

llm_judge_instructions.scoring_style: smooth (1-5 range per dimension, calibrated against severity matrix)

hard_failures_detected: list of HF IDs or empty list
failure_modes_detected: list of error_type strings from failure_mode_severity_mapping
